item.title: 使用 PHP 自动化 S3 + CloudFront CDN | Automating S3 + CloudFront CDN Using PHP
item.description:
如果您已阅读我的上一篇文章， 如何使用 AWS CloudFront 创建您自己的免费 CDN ，那么您希望您的网站资产拥有自己的几乎免费的 CDN。 但是通过aws cli工具手动维护所有资产可能很麻烦。 在我的上一篇文章中，我向您展示了如何使用aws sync和aws cp命令将文件从本地驱动器移动到 S3 存储桶，它是CloudFront CDN的后端。

现在我将向您展示如何使用 PHP 完全自动化这个过程。 本指南将主要关注PHP SDK 。

好了，废话不多说，开始吧！

我们需要做的第一件事是在您的网络服务器的根目录中安装 SDK。 如果您熟悉并且已经在您的网络服务器上安装了composer包，那么这应该非常简单。 由于本文不是关于如何实现 Composer 的指南，因此我假设您已经对此有所了解。 如果不是，请参阅作曲家网站。

通过 Composer 安装适用于 PHP 的 AWS 开发工具包

在您的网络服务器上的 BASH 会话中，导航您的网络根目录（无论您的 composer.json 文件位于何处）并输入：

 $ composer require aws/aws-sdk-php-resources

这应该在您的供应商文件夹中安装最新版本的AWS-SDK-PHP 。 如果您已经是作曲家用户，那么您的项目中就已经包含了autoload.php 。

测试您的安装

现在它已经安装好了，让我们尝试一个小测试。 首先我们需要创建一个配置。 您将需要您为网络服务器创建的 IAM 用户提供的安全凭证。 这包括您的Access Key和Secret Key 。 希望您将密钥保存在安全位置，因为您无法从 IAM 控制台再次查看它。 如果您忘记或丢失了它，您需要撤销旧密钥并生成新密钥。

您要避免做的一件事是将您的凭据硬编码到您的代码中。 这是一个安全漏洞，应尽可能避免。 相反，您可以使用密钥提供程序服务、环境变量或将它们存储在您的 SQL 数据库中。

以下是 PHP 中基本 AWS 配置的示例：

 $config = [ 'version' => 'latest', 'region' => 'us-east-1, 'credentials' => [ 'key' => 'YOUR_AWS_ACCESS_KEY', 'secret' => 'YOUR_AWS_SECREY_KEY' ] ];

同样，不要在此处对您的凭据进行硬编码。 就个人而言，我从 MySQL 数据库中提取它们并将它们分配给一个变量，但还有很多其他方法可以存储它们。

好的，让我们开始吧。 我们将从简单的事情开始。 让我们包含 AWS S3 类并列出我们账户上的所有 S3 存储桶：

 <?php require 'vendor/autoload.php'; use Aws\S3\S3Client; use Aws\Exception\AwsException; try { $s3 = new S3Client($config); $buckets = $s3->listBuckets(); foreach ($buckets as $bucket) { echo $bucket['Name']."\n"; } } catch (S3Exception $e) { echo $e->getMessage(); } ?>

好的，这很基本，但希望您的代码打印出您的 S3 存储桶列表。 理想情况下，您已经知道要将文件上传到哪个存储桶，因此请继续将对象上传到您首选的 S3 存储桶。

但首先，我想详细说明 AWS 的简单存储服务 (S3)的工作原理。 这个概念可能需要一些时间来适应。 如果您熟悉面向对象编程，那么您就会知道对象是用于操作数据的属性和方法的集合。 好吧，S3 的工作方式非常相似。 因此，当您想要引用“文件”或“S3 对象”时，您将引用它的key ，就像您以编程方式处理对象一样。 话虽如此，AWS S3 将其称为key ，而不是file path 。 键是 S3 存储桶根目录的相对路径，前面没有斜杠。

上传 S3 对象

好的，让我们将图像文件上传到我们的 S3 存储桶：

 <?php require 'vendor/autoload.php'; use Aws\S3\S3Client; use Aws\Exception\AwsException; try { $s3 = new S3Client($config); $fileData = file_get_contents("/path/to/your/image.jpg"); $mimeType = mime_content_type("/path/to/your/image.jpg"); $s3->putObject([ 'Key' => 'images/image.jpg', 'Body' => $fileData, 'ContentType' => $mimeType ]); } catch (S3Exception $e) { echo $e->getMessage(); } ?>

现在您应该能够通过您的CloudFront Distribution Domain Name访问您的对象。 如果您对此不确定，请参阅我之前的文章。 您的完整 URL 路径将如下所示：

 https://sdf934r3iruihi3u4h.cloudfront.net/images/image.jpg

使 CloudFront 缓存中的对象无效

存储在 S3 存储桶中的文件会自动缓存在 CloudFront 边缘服务器的缓存中。 除非您想等待 TTL（生存时间）到期，否则您可能希望使新上传的对象无效以在浏览器中获取最新版本。 以下是在 PHP-SDK 中的操作方法：

 <?php use Aws\CloudFront\CloudFrontClient; use Aws\Exception\AwsException; try { $cf = new CloudFrontClient($config); $cf->createInvalidation([ 'DistributionId' => "YOUR_CLOUDFRONT_DISTID', 'InvalidationBatch' => [ 'CallerReference' => "UNIQUE_CALLER_IDENTIFIER', 'Paths' => [ 'Items' => [ 'images/images.jpg' ], 'Quantity' => 1 ] ] ]); } catch (AwsException $e) { echo $e->getAwsErrorMessage(); } ?>

此代码将使 image.jpg 的缓存版本无效，并从您的 S3 存储桶中提取当前版本。 这主要用于记录目的。

高级示例：连接和缩小 JavaScript 代码

好吧，让我们认真一点。 在此示例中，我将向您展示如何自动连接您的 JavaScript 代码、缩小它，然后将其上传到您的 CDN。

您首先需要安装 Mathias Mullie 的Minify库：

 $ composer require matthiasmullie/minify

这是代码：

 <?php use MatthiasMullie\Minify; use Aws\Resource\Aws; use Aws\CloudFront\CloudFrontClient; use Aws\S3\S3Client; // Instantiate the Minify/JS class $m = new Minify\JS; // Iterate through our array of JavaScript files and concatenate them foreach ($jsFiles as $file) { $m->add($file); } // Minify the code $out = $m->minify(); // Upload the minified code to our S3 bucket try { $s3 = new S3Client($config); $s3->putObject([ 'Key' => 'js/main.min.js', 'Body' => $out, 'ContentType' => 'text/javascript' ]); } catch (S3Exception $e) { echo $e->getMessage(); } // Invalidate the existing file try { $cf = new CloudFrontClient($config); $cf->createInvalidation([ 'DistributionId' => "YOUR_CLOUDFRONT_DISTID', 'InvalidationBatch' => [ 'CallerReference' => "UNIQUE_CALLER_IDENTIFIER', 'Paths' => [ 'Items' => [ 'js/main.min.js' ], 'Quantity' => 1 ] ] ]); } catch (AwsException $e) { echo $e->getAwsErrorMessage(); }

正如您所看到的，此设置对于通过自动化通常需要大量时间手动完成的事情来加速您的网页设计过程非常强大。 您也可以使用相同的代码连接和上传 CSS。 只需调用 Minify/CSS 而不是 Minify/JS。

我希望您发现本指南有用！ 如需更多重要信息，请访问我们的博客。

另请参阅：如何使用 AWS CloudFront 创建您自己的免费 CDN 。



原文:

If you’ve read my last article, How to Use AWS CloudFront to Create Your Own Free CDN, you hopefully have your own practically-free CDN for your website assets. But maintaining all of your assets manually via the aws cli tool can be cumbersome. In my last article, I showed you how to use the aws sync and aws cp commands to move files from your local drive to your S3 bucket, which is the back-end for your CloudFront CDN.

Now I’m going to show you how to fully automate this process using PHP. Amazon Web Services has SDKs for several programming platforms, such as C++, Go, Java, JavaScript, .NET, NodeJS, PHP, Python and Ruby. This guide will largely focus on the PHP SDK.

Ok, enough babble, let’s et started!

The first thing we need to do is install the SDK in the root directory of your webserver. If you are familiar with and already have composer packages installed on your webserver, then this should be pretty straight-forward. Since this article is not a guide on how to implement composer, I’m going to assume you already have your bearings on it. If, not, please refer to the Composer Website.

Install the AWS SDK for PHP via Composer

In a BASH session on your webserver, navigate your your web root directory (wherever your composer.json file is located) and enter:

$ composer require aws/aws-sdk-php-resources

This should install the latest version of the AWS-SDK-PHP in your vendor folder. And if you’re already a composer user, you’ll already have your autoload.php included in your project.

Test Your Installation

Now that it’s installed, let’s try a little test. First we need to create a configuration. You’ll need your security credentials from the IAM user you created for your webserver. This includes your Access Key and Secret Key. Hopefully, you have your secret key saved in a secure location, as you cannot view it again from the IAM console. If you’ve forgotten or lost it, you’ll need to revoke your old keys and generate new ones.

One thing you want to avoid doing is hard-coding your credentials into your code. This is a security vulnerability and should be avoided, if possible. Instead, you can use a key provider service, environment variables or store them in your SQL database.

Here’s an example of a basic AWS configuration in PHP:

$config = [
      'version' => 'latest',
      'region' => 'us-east-1,
      'credentials' => [
         'key' => 'YOUR_AWS_ACCESS_KEY',
         'secret' => 'YOUR_AWS_SECREY_KEY'
   ]
];

Again, do not hard-code your credentials here. Personally, I pull them from a MySQL database and assign them to a variable, but there are plenty of other methods to store them.

Ok, let’s get right into it. We’ll start of with something simple. Let’s include the AWS S3 class and list all of the S3 buckets on our account:

<?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
use Aws\S3\S3Client;
use Aws\Exception\AwsException;

try {
   $s3 = new S3Client($config);

   $buckets = $s3->listBuckets();

   foreach ($buckets as $bucket) {
      echo $bucket['Name']."\n";
   }
}
catch (S3Exception $e)
{
   echo $e->getMessage();
}
?>

Ok, that’s pretty basic, but hopefully your code printed out a list of your S3 buckets. Ideally, you’ll already know which bucket you want to upload your files to, so let continue with uploading objects to your preferred S3 bucket.

But first, I’d like to elaborate on how AWS’s Simple Storage Service (S3) works. Amazon S3 calls “files” stored in your bucket objects. This concept can take some getting used to. If you’re familiar with Object-oriented Programming , then you know that objects are a collection of properties and methods to manipulate data. Well, S3 works in a very similar capacity. So when you want to refer to a “file” or “S3 Object,” you’ll refer to it’s key , just as you would programmatically with an object. That being said, instead of file path , AWS S3 calls it a key. The key is a relative path to your S3 bucket’s root, without the preceding slash.

Upload an S3 Object

Ok, let’s upload an image file to our S3 bucket:

<?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
use Aws\S3\S3Client;
use Aws\Exception\AwsException;

try {
   $s3 = new S3Client($config);

   $fileData = file_get_contents("/path/to/your/image.jpg");
   $mimeType = mime_content_type("/path/to/your/image.jpg");

   $s3->putObject([
      'Key' => 'images/image.jpg',
      'Body' => $fileData,
      'ContentType' => $mimeType
   ]);

}
catch (S3Exception $e)
{
   echo $e->getMessage();
}
?>

Now you should be able to access your object via your CloudFront Distribution Domain Name. If you’re unsure about this, please refer to my previous article. Your full URL path will look something like this:

https://sdf934r3iruihi3u4h.cloudfront.net/images/image.jpg

Invalidate Object from the CloudFront Cache

Files stored in your S3 bucket are automatically cached on the CloudFront edge server’s cache. Unless you want to wait for the TTL (time to live) to expire, you may want to invalidate newly-uploaded objects to get the latest version in your browser. Here’s how to do it in the PHP-SDK:

<?php
use Aws\CloudFront\CloudFrontClient;
use Aws\Exception\AwsException;

try {
   $cf = new CloudFrontClient($config);
   $cf->createInvalidation([
      'DistributionId' => "YOUR_CLOUDFRONT_DISTID',
      'InvalidationBatch' => [
         'CallerReference' => "UNIQUE_CALLER_IDENTIFIER',
         'Paths' => [
            'Items' => [
               'images/images.jpg'
             ],
            'Quantity' => 1
         ]
      ]
   ]);
}
catch (AwsException $e)
{
   echo $e->getAwsErrorMessage();
}
?>

This code will invalidate the cached version of image.jpg and pull the current version from your S3 bucket. The CallerReference property is a unique identifier you need to come up with to identify the source of the request. This is mainly used for logging purposes.

Advanced Example: Concatenating and Minifying JavaScript Code

Ok, let’s get serious. In this example, I’ll show you how to automate concatenating your JavaScript code, minifying it, and then uploading it to your CDN.

You’ll first need to install Mathias Mullie’s Minify library:

$ composer require matthiasmullie/minify

And here’s the code:

<?php
use MatthiasMullie\Minify;
use Aws\Resource\Aws;
use Aws\CloudFront\CloudFrontClient;
use Aws\S3\S3Client;

// Instantiate the Minify/JS class
$m = new Minify\JS;

// Iterate through our array of JavaScript files and concatenate them
foreach ($jsFiles as $file)
{
   $m->add($file);
}

// Minify the code
$out = $m->minify();

// Upload the minified code to our S3 bucket

try {
   $s3 = new S3Client($config);

   $s3->putObject([
      'Key' => 'js/main.min.js',
      'Body' => $out,
      'ContentType' => 'text/javascript'
   ]);

}
catch (S3Exception $e)
{
   echo $e->getMessage();
}

// Invalidate the existing file
try {
   $cf = new CloudFrontClient($config);
   $cf->createInvalidation([
      'DistributionId' => "YOUR_CLOUDFRONT_DISTID',
      'InvalidationBatch' => [
         'CallerReference' => "UNIQUE_CALLER_IDENTIFIER',
         'Paths' => [
            'Items' => [
               'js/main.min.js'
             ],
            'Quantity' => 1
         ]
      ]
   ]);
}
catch (AwsException $e)
{
   echo $e->getAwsErrorMessage();
}

As you can see, this setup can be very powerful for speeding up your web design process by automating things that would normally take a lot of time to do manually. You can use this same code to concatenate and upload CSS as well. Just call Minify/CSS instead of Minify/JS.

I hope you found this guide useful! For more great information, please visit our Blog.

See also: How to Use AWS CloudFront to Create Your Own Free CDN.
item.author: designly

item.title: 如何使用 AWS CloudFront 创建您自己的免费 CDN | How to Use AWS CloudFront to Create Your Own Free CDN
item.description:
它们被 Facebook、Amazon、Twitter 等大型平台使用。传统上，它们只出现在拥有大量钱包的公司的市场中，但现在，您可以以可承受的价格拥有自己的 CDN 解决方案。

在过去的几年里，我使用CloudFlare来缓存我的网站并加快交付速度——现在我仍然这样做。 但最近我发现了Amazon Web Service的CloudFront产品。 与许多 AWS 服务一样，CloudFront 也有免费套餐！ 免费套餐每月最多允许1TB的传输、 10,000,000 个 HTTP 或 HTTPS 请求以及2,000,000 个 CloudFront 函数调用。 超额费用只是几美分。 过去，此类服务仅适用于每天有数百万个请求的大型站点，但亚马逊已将这项服务提供给任何人——您只需要一个 AWS 帐户和存档的信用卡，但您仅当您超过免费套餐配额时，才会从信用卡中收取费用。

所以，事不宜迟，让我们开始吧！ 本指南假定您已经拥有一个 AWS 账户并已登录。

步骤 1：创建 S3 存储桶

与 Google Drive 或 DropBox 等服务不同，S3 将存储的文件视为对象。 这使您可以更好地控制文件管理，例如版本控制、删除保护和加密选项。 在本教程中，我们将创建一个没有花里胡哨的标准 S3 存储桶。

好的，一旦您登录AWS Console ，点击左上角的Services图标，然后点击Storage ，然后点击S3 。


单击服务 > 存储 > S3

接下来，单击Create Bucket并输入Bucket Name 。 您选择的名称在所有 AWS 中必须是唯一的，因此您可能需要反复尝试，直到找到一个有效的名称。 您应该选择一个符合 DNS 标准的名称，因为该名称将成为您的 URI 的一部分，以便从 Web 访问存储桶。

现在选择您的AWS Region ，它应该与您当前使用的任何其他 AWS 服务相同。 如果这是您的第一个 AWS 资源，请选择地理位置接近您居住地的区域。


创建一个新存储桶，然后输入存储桶名称并选择您的 AWS 区域

接下来，向下滚动到阻止所有公共访问复选框并取消选中它。 此存储桶旨在供公众访问，因此我们可以取消选中此选项。 然后选中“我确认”复选框以继续。


取消选中阻止所有公共访问并选中“我确认”

第 2 步：设置 AWS CloudFront

现在我们有了一个 S3 存储桶，我们可以设置 AWS CloudFront，它将使用该存储桶作为 CDN 的后端存储。

单击服务图标，然后选择网络和内容交付 > CloudFront 。


单击服务 > 网络和内容交付 > CloudFront

在 CloudFront 控制台中，单击Create Distribution 。 然后单击Origin Domain并选择您新创建的 S3 存储桶。


单击创建分配，然后单击源域并选择您的存储桶

接下来，向下滚动到Viewer部分并在Viewer protocol policy下选择Redirect HTTP to HTTPS 。


在查看器协议策略下选择将 HTTP 重定向到 HTTPS

将所有其他设置保留为默认状态，然后向下滚动到底部并单击Create Distribution 。

现在将分发域名复制并粘贴到您以后可以引用它的地方。 这是您的新 CDN 的基本 URL。

第 3 步：创建 IAM 用户/组来管理您的 CDN

现在我们需要创建一个具有适当权限的服务帐户用户来管理您的新 CDN。 在服务图标旁边的搜索栏中，输入Iam ，然后单击IAM下的用户。


搜索 Iam，然后单击用户

接下来，单击添加用户并输入用户名。 然后选中Select AWS credential type下标记为Access key的框。


单击添加用户并输入用户名，然后检查访问密钥

现在您需要将新用户添加到组中。 我们要创建组。 将组命名为您喜欢的任何名称，并在搜索栏中输入S3并检查AmazonS3FullAcess 。 这将使该组可以完全访问您的 S3 存储桶以管理对象。


单击将用户添加到组，然后搜索 S3 并检查 AmazonS3FullAccess

现在搜索cloudfront并检查CloudFrontFullAccess 。 然后单击创建组。


搜索 cloudfront 并检查 CloudFrontFullAccess 然后单击 Create Group

单击下一步，跳过添加标签，然后单击创建用户。 现在复制并粘贴您的访问密钥 ID和秘密访问密钥，并将它们存储在安全的地方。 关闭此屏幕后，您将无法再次看到您的密钥！


复制您的访问密钥 ID 和秘密访问密钥

步骤 4：安装 AWS 命令​​行界面

我们有自己的免费 CDN！ 耶！ 现在我们需要能够将文件上传到 S3 存储桶并控制我们的 CloudFront 缓存。 所以我们需要安装 AWS CLI。 我提供了在MacOS或Linux上安装它的方法。 我从来没有在Windows上使用过它，因为……嗯……我讨厌 Windows！ 非常抱歉。

在 MacOS 上安装 AWS CLI

假设您已经在 Mac 上安装了Homebrew ，请运行以下命令：

 $ brew update $ brew install awscli

在 Linux 上安装 AWS CLI

安装完成后，您可以通过运行以下命令测试它是否安装正确：

它应该输出如下内容：

 aws-cli/2.1.33 Python/3.8.8 Linux/5.4.0-70-generic exe/x86_64.ubuntu.20 prompt/off

接下来，我们需要使用您仔细记下的访问密钥和私钥登录。 为此，我们将运行 aws configure：

 $ aws configure AWS Access Key ID [None]: YOUR_ACCESS_KEY AWS Secret Access Key [None]: YOUR_SECRET_KEY Default region name [None]: eg: us-east-1 Default output format [None]: LEAVE_BLANK

如果一切顺利，您现在已登录并可以开始将文件上传到您的 S3 存储桶！

有两种方法可以将文件上传到 CDN。 您可以使用 aws s3 cp 命令或 aws s3 sync 命令。 就个人而言，我更喜欢 sync 命令，因为它的工作方式与rsync非常相似。 因此，在此示例中，我将向您展示如何将本地图像目录同步到您的 CloudFront CDN：

 aws s3 sync /Users/johndoe/httpd/images/ s3://designly-test-cdn/images --delete --exclude ".*"

如果你熟悉rsync那么这个命令应该看起来很熟悉。 您很可能不想上传任何操作系统隐藏文件，例如 .DS_Store。

好的，你做到了！ 现在，您的网站资产拥有一个全球联合 CDN！ 您应该会在您的网站上看到加载时间的巨大增长。

要访问您的文件，请记住我之前要求您记下的分发域名。 在我的示例中，它是https://dmcgr6zwv04i4.cloudfront.net 。 因此，要访问我们刚刚上传的特定图像文件，您需要输入https://dmcgr6zwv04i4.cloudfront.net /images/your-image-file.jpg。

使您的 CDN 缓存失效

如果您经常进行大量开发和更改文件，则可能需要使缓存中的某些文件无效。 为此，请运行以下 awscli 命令：

 $ aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id YOUR_DISTRIBUTION_ID --paths "/*"

其中 --distribution-id 是您的 CloudFront分配 ID ，而 --paths 是您要使其无效的特定文件。 如果您输入“/*”，它将使所有内容无效。

嗯，就是这样。 我希望您发现本教程和我一样有用！ 如需更多重要信息，请访问我们的博客。

另请参阅：使用 PHP 自动化 S3 + CloudFront CDN 。



原文:

CDN stands for Content Delivery Network. They are used by large platforms such as Facebook, Amazon, Twitter, etc. Traditionally, they were only in the market of companies with deep wallets, but now-a-days, you can have your own CDN solution for an affordable price.

In years past, I used CloudFlare to cache my websites and speed up delivery — and I still do. But recently I discovered Amazon Web Service’s CloudFront product. Like many AWS services, CloudFront has a free tier! The free tier allows up to 1TB of transfer out, 10,000,000 HTTP or HTTPS Requests , and 2,000,000 CloudFront Function Invocations per month. And the overage charges are just pennies. In the past, this type of service was only available to large-scale sites with millions of requests per day, but Amazon has made this service available to anyone — all you need is an AWS account and a credit card on file, but you’re credit card will only be charged if you go over the free-tier quota.

So, without further ado, let’s get started! This guide assumes you already have an AWS account and are logged in.

Step 1: Create an S3 Bucket

S3 stands for Simple Storage Service. Unlike services, such as Google Drive or DropBox, S3 treats files stored as objects. This gives you greater control over management of your files, such as versioning, deletion protection and encryption option. In this tutorial, we are just going to create a standard S3 bucket with no bells and whistles.

Ok, once you’re logged into the AWS Console, click on the Services icon in the upper left corner, then click Storage , then click S3.


Click Services > Storage > S3

Next, click Create Bucket and enter a Bucket Name. The name you choose must be unique amongst all of AWS, so you may have to play around until you find one that works. You should choose a name that is DNS-compliant because this name will be part of your URI to access the bucket from the web.

Now choose your AWS Region , which should be the same as any other AWS services you currently use. If this if your first AWS resource, choose a region that geographically close to where you live.


Create a New Bucket and then enter a Bucket Name and choose your AWS Region

Next, scroll down to the Block all Public Access checkbox and uncheck it. This bucket is intended to be accessible to the public so we can uncheck this option. Then check the “I Acknowledge” checkbox to continue.


Uncheck Block all pubic access and check “I Acknowledge”

Step 2: Setup AWS CloudFront

Now that we have an S3 bucket, we can setup AWS CloudFront, which will use that bucket as back-end storage for your CDN.

Click the Services icon and then choose Network & Content Delivery > CloudFront.


Click Services > Network & Content Delivery > CloudFront

Once in the CloudFront console, click Create Distribution. Then click on Origin Domain and select your newly-created S3 bucket.


Click Create Distribution and then click Origin Domain and select your bucket

Next, scroll down to the Viewer section and select Redirect HTTP to HTTPS under Viewer protocol policy.


Select Redirect HTTP to HTTPS under Viewer protocol Policy

Leave all the other settings in their default state and then scroll down to the bottom and click Create Distribution.

Now copy and paste the Distribution domain name into a place where you can reference it later. This is your new CDN’s base url.

Step 3: Create an IAM User/Group to Manage your CDN

Now we need to create a service account user with appropriate permissions to manage your new CDN. In the Search Bar next to the Services icon type in Iam and then click Users under IAM.


Search for Iam and then click Users

Next, click Add Users and enter a username. Then check the box labeled Access key under Select AWS credential type.


Click Add Users and enter a Username then check Access Key

Now you’ll need to add your new user to a group. We’re going to Create Group. Name the group whatever you like and the type S3 in the search baar and check AmazonS3FullAcess. This will give this group full access to your S3 bucket for managing objects.


Click Add User To Group and then search for S3 and check AmazonS3FullAccess

Now search for cloudfront and check CloudFrontFullAccess. Then click Create Group.


Search for cloudfront and check CloudFrontFullAccess then click Create Group

Click next, skip adding tags and then click Create User. Now copy and paste your Access key ID and Secret access key and store them in a safe place. Once you close this screen you will not be able to see your secret key again!


Copy your Access Key ID and Secret access key

Step 4: Install the AWS Command Line Interface

We have our own free CDN! Yay! Now we need to be able to upload files to the S3 bucket and control our CloudFront cache. So we need to install the AWS CLI. I offer methods to install this on MacOS or Linux. I’ve never used it on Windows because… well… I hate Windows! So sorry.

Install AWS CLI on MacOS

Assuming you alread have Homebrew installed on your Mac, run the following commands:

$ brew update
$ brew install awscli

Install AWS CLI on Linux

Debian/Ubuntu

$ curl "https://awscli.amazonaws.com/awscli-exe-linux-x86_64.zip" -o "awscliv2.zip"
$ unzip awscliv2.zip
$ cd aws
$ sudo ./install

Once the installation completes you can test if it installed properly by running:

It should output something like:

aws-cli/2.1.33 Python/3.8.8 Linux/5.4.0-70-generic exe/x86_64.ubuntu.20 prompt/off

Next, we need to login using the Access key and Private key you so carefully wrote down. To do this, we’ll run aws configure:

$ aws configure
AWS Access Key ID [None]: YOUR_ACCESS_KEY
AWS Secret Access Key [None]: YOUR_SECRET_KEY
Default region name [None]: e.g: us-east-1
Default output format [None]: LEAVE_BLANK

If all goes well, you are now logged in and may begin uploading files to your S3 bucket!

There are two ways to get your file uploaded to your CDN. You can use the aws s3 cp command or the aws s3 sync command. Personally, I prefer the sync command as it works very similarly to rsync. So in this example, I’m going to show you how to sync a local images directory to your CloudFront CDN:

aws s3 sync /Users/johndoe/httpd/images/ s3://designly-test-cdn/images --delete --exclude ".*"

If you’re familiar with rsync then this command should look very familiar. The --delete option tells the command to delete any files in the target directory that no longer exists. The --exclude directive tells sync to ignore any files that begin with a dot. Most likely, you won’t want to upload any operating system hidden files like .DS_Store.

Ok, you did it! You now have a globally-syndicated CDN for your website’s assets! You should see huge gains in loading times on your site.

To access your files, remember the Distribution domain name I asked you to note earlier. In my example it was https://dmcgr6zwv04i4.cloudfront.net. So to get to a specific image file we just uploaded you would enter https://dmcgr6zwv04i4.cloudfront.net/images/your-image-file.jpg.

Invalidating Your CDN Cache

CloudFront automatically caches your S3 files at the CloudFront edge server for lightning-fast delivery. If you are doing a lot go development and changing files regularly, it may be necessary to invalidate certain files from the cache. To do this run the following awscli command:

$ aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id YOUR_DISTRIBUTION_ID --paths "/*"

Where --distribution-id is your CloudFront Distribution ID and --paths is the specific files you want to invalidate. If you enter "/*", it will invalidate everything.

Well, that’s it. I hope you found this tutorial as useful as I have! For more great information, please visit our Blog.

See also: Automating S3 + CloudFront CDN Using PHP.
item.author: designly

item.title: 本周讨论和评论 - v5 | Discussion and Comment of the Week - v5
item.description:
这份每周综述重点介绍了我们认为本周最深思熟虑和/或最有趣的讨论。 我们还将在每一期中突出一个特别酷的评论。 因此，我们希望鼓励人们参与讨论并奖励那些在社区中发起或参与对话的人。 毕竟，社区是通过人们在其中互动而成为可能的。

本周讨论

我们想把焦点放在@shahednasser的#discuss帖子上，主题是“你希望什么工具是开源的？ ”：

阅读此类帖子的评论部分以了解不同的软件品味总是很有趣。 很多人都希望Notion和Adob​​e套件是 OSS，并且很多人都希望使用开源替代方案。

我想支持@moopet的建议：

那不是很好吗！ 此外，对于那些还不知道的人，DEV 所基于的平台 Forem 是开源的！ 在GitHub 上查看，如果您对我们如何改进技术有想法，请考虑发起讨论。

本周评论

对于本周的选择，我不得不放弃@inhuofficial ，他对@nombrekeff的出色提示“如何告诉初级开发人员他们所做的事情是错误的？ ”做出了一些非常周到的建议。

他们在这里强调的要点非常有帮助：询问人们他们喜欢如何接受批评+给他们建设性的理由，以便他们知道为什么他们错了并且可以改进。 但除了建议本身之外，它的表述方式令人难以置信的自我意识和同情心。 引用向我指出此评论的 Forem 团队成员 ( @erinposting )：

越多的经理就这些事情进行坦诚的对话，世界上所有的年轻人都会过得更好。

如此真实！

你的选择是什么？

这个社区里有很多很棒的讨论和评论。 这些只是我们选择强调的几个。 我敦促大家在下面的评论中分享您最喜欢的评论和/或对过去一周的讨论。 如果你愿意，请给作者一个@mention——它可能会让他们感觉良好。 💚 💚



原文:

This weekly roundup highlights what we believe to be the most thoughtful and/or interesting discussion of the week. We're also be highlighting one particularly cool comment in each installment. 🙌

The DEV Community is particularly special because of the kind and thoughtful discussions happening between community members. As such, we want to encourage folks to participate in discussions and reward those who are initiating or taking part in conversations across the community. After all, a community is made possible by the people interacting inside it.

Discussion of the Week

We'd like to put the spotlight on @shahednasser's #discuss post with the topic "What Tool Do You Wish Was Open Source?":

It's always fun reading through the comments section on posts like these to get a sense of different software tastes out there. Plenty of folks out there wishing Notion and the Adobe suite were OSS, and lots of folks chiming in with open source alternatives to each.

I'd like to second @moopet's suggestion:

Wouldn't that be nice! 😀 One can wish...

Also, for those that don't already know, the platform that DEV is built on, Forem, is open source! Check it out on GitHub and if you have ideas for how we might improve our tech, consider starting up a discussion.

Comment of the Week

For this week's pick, I gotta give it up for @inhuofficial who responded with some incredibly thoughtful advice to @nombrekeff's excellent prompt "How to tell a Junior Dev that what they've done is wrong?"

The points they highlighted here are seriously helpful: asking folks how they like to receive criticism + giving them constructive reasons so that they know why they were wrong and can improve. But beyond the advice itself, the way it was stated felt so incredibly self-aware and empathetic. To quote a Forem team member (@erinposting) who pointed this comment out to me:

The more managers that have candid conversations about this stuff, the better off all the juniors of the world will be.

So true!

What are your picks?

There's loads of great discussions and comments floating about in this community. These are just a few we chose to highlight. 🙂

I urge you all to share your favorite comment and/or discussion of the past week below in the comments. And if you're up for it, give the author an @mention — it'll probably make 'em feel good. 💚
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: React：使用本机对话框制作模态弹出窗口 | React: Using native dialogs to make a modal popup
item.description:
大约两年前，我写了一篇文章，我仍然为之感到自豪，名为“使用门户制作模态弹出窗口”。 它利用了门户和惰性。 当时并没有真正的好方法来在其他所有内容之上显示模式弹出窗口并在其中捕获焦点。

自最初的帖子以来已经过去了两年，虽然我认为代码仍然有效并且使用的技术仍然值得了解，但现在有一种更好的方式来编写模态代码。 对话框元素自 2014 年以来就已经存在，但直到最近才达到完全的浏览器兼容性（IE 除外）。

让我们看看如何使用对话框元素来实现这一点。

对话框元素

什么是对话框元素？ 默认情况下，元素的内容是隐藏的，但通过将open属性设置为 true 或通过调用其显示方法show()或showModal()可以显示它们。

然而，直接设置open属性并不是最佳实践，即使可以，但最好调用 show 方法。 使用它有什么好处？ 我确信有很多理由使用对话而不是制作自己的解决方案，但我将重点关注三个。

  • 本机元素不需要大型库或导入，从而加快您的应用程序。
  • 当浏览器发布一个元素时，它会为您处理可访问性，它是按照 a11y 标准构建的。
  • 逻辑几乎就在那里，您不必弄清楚如何使交互发生，它们就可以工作。

有什么缺点吗？ 是的。 嗯，有点。 该元素没有内置动画并使用display: none ，这是众所周知的难以制作动画的。 在我看来，奇怪的是，它还缺乏一种接近的方法。

这些问题几乎感觉像是破坏交易，但有两种方法可以解决它们，我将在我们继续进行时展示。

通用组件

我不会花太长时间浏览我编写的代码，请随意阅读并在评论中提出问题，但我会在这里快速介绍一下，然后解释我对上面提到的问题的解决方案.

首先我使用的是Sass和CSS 模块，如果你看过我之前的一些帖子，你会看到我曾经使用样式化组件，虽然我认为它们有自己的位置，但我更喜欢使用 CSS 模块。

代码做了一些事情，它有在事件监听器中使用的引用（我将在捕获事件部分详细介绍它们），根据需要应用模块中的类，在打开时触发showModal()方法设置并将 html ( 真正的jsx ) 绘制到屏幕上。 就是这样。

添加动画

如果我们的 modal 只是在被召唤时出现，在被解雇时消失，那没关系，但它会缺乏润色的感觉。 在我的示例中，您会注意到有轻微的淡入和上移效果。 让我们看看我们是如何做到的。

我们有两个关键帧动画，一个叫做 show，一个叫做 hide。 他们只是有一个开始位置和一个结束位置。

 @keyframes show { from { opacity : 0 ; transform : translateY ( min ( 100px , 5vh )); } to { opacity : 1 ; transform : translateY ( 0% ); } } @keyframes hide { from { opacity : 1 ; transform : translateY ( 0% ); } to { opacity : 0 ; transform : translateY ( min ( 100px , 5vh )); } }

要应用动画，我们必须知道模态是打开还是关闭，这是我们设置类名的地方。我们将始终应用模态类，但我们只会在模态未打开时应用关闭类.

 // work out which classes should be applied to the dialog element const dialogClasses = useMemo (() => { const _arr = [ styles [ " modal " ]]; if ( ! open ) _arr . push ( styles [ " modal--closing " ]); return _arr . join ( " " ); }, [ open ]);

因为当我们移除 open 属性时模态框没有关闭，我们可以假设模态框是[open]但具有关闭类。
我们使用它在模态打开时应用显示动画，并在模态打开但具有关闭类时隐藏动画。 我们还使用forwards作为动画方向，所以当动画结束时，我们会停留在最后一帧。

 & [ open ] { animation : show 250ms cubic-bezier ( 0 .4 , 0 , 0 .2 , 1 ) forwards ; & .modal--closing { animation : hide 150ms cubic-bezier ( 0 .4 , 0 , 0 .2 , 1 ) forwards ; } }

您可能已经注意到，通过这些更改，我们的模态动画消失了，但实际上并没有关闭，如果您这样做了，那么您是对的并且非常精明。 下一节将向您展示我们如何使用动画来真正关闭模态框。

捕捉事件

我们在这里要看三件事。 其中两个是添加事件，第三个也是添加一个事件，但有一些技巧可以让我们用 js 关闭模式，即使没有方法。

关闭背景点击

我们可以给对话框元素添加一个点击事件，但是没有办法区分点击背景和点击模态框。 解决这个问题的最简单方法是在模态框内放置一个容器并让它占据整个模态框。 现在，当我们在模态框内单击时，目标将是容器，当我们在模态框外单击时，目标将是对话框。

 // Eventlistener: trigger onclose when click outside const onClick = useCallback ( ({ target }) => { const { current : el } = modalRef ; if ( target === el && ! locked ) onClose (); }, [ locked , onClose ] );

在 Escape 上制作动画

默认情况下，按下 Escape 会关闭对话框，这是我们想要发生的，但不幸的是我们的动画会随之发生，所以让我们捕获 Escape Press 并自己处理它。

 // Eventlistener: trigger onclose when cancel detected const onCancel = useCallback ( ( e ) => { e . preventDefault (); if ( ! locked ) onClose (); }, [ locked , onClose ] );

用 JS 结束

到目前为止，我们实现的两个事件侦听器都调用了 onClose 函数，正如我们之前讨论的那样，它不会关闭模式，它只是将其动画化。 为了把这个动画变成一个关闭的方法，我们需要两个东西，hacky JSX 和一个事件监听器。

哈奇 JSX

对话框元素带有一个很酷的form集成，带有方法dialog的form内带有cancel值的button将关闭其父对话框。 这是一个很好的功能，再加上使用 CSS 隐藏元素的能力，使用tabIndex将它们从 Tab 键顺序中取出，以及使用 JS 单击按钮的能力，我认为我们有我们的 close 方法。

 < form method = "dialog" className = { styles [ " modal__close " ] } > < button ref = { closeRef } tabIndex = { - 1 } value = "cancel" > Cancel </ button > </ form >

现在我们只需要访问我们的closeRef并在其上运行 click 方法，我们的对话框就会关闭。

事件监听器

有了关闭对话框的方法，我们可以将注意力转向在关闭动画完成时调用该方法，幸运的是，当动画完成时它会触发一个事件。

我们需要检查我们的 Modal 元素是否open ，但如果它没有打开，我们可以单击关闭按钮，就像对话框关闭一样。

 // Eventlistener: trigger close click on anim end const onAnimEnd = useCallback (() => { if ( open ) return ; const { current : close } = closeRef ; close . click (); }, [ open ]);

结束的想法

网络每天都在变化和发展，跟上正在发生的事情是令人兴奋的。 感谢您的阅读，我希望您喜欢了解对话框元素以及如何在 React 中使用它。 如果您有任何问题，请随时在评论中提出，我将非常乐意回答。

正如我在顶部所说，我真的为我关于这个主题的原始帖子感到自豪，所以如果你还没有阅读它并且你有兴趣，请前往那里看看。

如果你想在 Dev 之外与我联系，这里是我的twitter和linkedin来打个招呼😊。



原文:

Around two years ago I wrote a post, that I'm still very proud of, called "Using portals to make a modal popup". It made use of portals and inert. At the time there wasn't really a good way to display a modal popup on top of everything else and trap focus within it.

Two years have passed since the original post and, whilst I would argue the code is still valid and the techniques used are still worth knowing, there is now a better way to code a modal. The dialog element has been around since 2014ish but it has only recently reached full browser compatibility (excusing IE).

Let's look at how we can use the dialog element to make this.

The Dialog Element

What is the Dialog Element? The <dialog> element is a native html element made with the sole purpose of being popover content. By default the contents of the element are hidden but by setting the open attribute to true or by calling either of its show methods show() or showModal() they can be revealed.

It is not, however, best practice to set the open attribute directly, even though it is possible, but rather calling a show method is preferred. show() makes the dialog appear but leaves the rest of the page interactable this is great for making toast notifications. showModal() opens the dialog in the centre of the screen and makes all other content inaccessible.

What are the advantages of using it? I'm sure there are numerous reasons to use dialog over making your own solution but I'll focus on three.

  • Native elements do not require large libraries or imports, speeding up your app.
  • Accessibility is handled for you, when a browser ships an element it is built to a11y standards.
  • The logic is pretty much there, you don't have to work out how to make the interactions happen they just work.

Are there any drawbacks? Yes. Well, sort of. The element does not have animations built in and uses display: none which is famously hard to animate from. It also lacks, strangely in my opinion, a close method.

These issues almost feel like deal breakers but there are ways around them both, which I will show as we go on.

The general component

I'm not going to spend too long going through the code I've written, feel free to read it and ask questions in the comments, but I'll give a quick run down here and then explain my solutions to issues I mentioned above.

First of all I'm using Sass and CSS Modules if you've seen some of my earlier posts you'll have seen I used to use styled-components and whilst I think they have their place I'm much happier using CSS Modules.

The code does a few things, it has references to use in event listeners (I'll go more into them in the capturing events section), applies classes from the modules as they're required, triggers the showModal() method when open is set and draws the html (jsx really) to the screen. That's it.

Adding animations

If our modal just appeared when summoned and disappeared when dismissed that would be ok but it would lack the feeling of polish. In my example you'll have noticed there is a slight fade in and move up effect. Let's look and how we did it.

We have two keyframe animations one called show and one called hide. They simply have a start position and an end position.

@keyframes show{ from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(min(100px, 5vh)); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0%); }
} @keyframes hide{ from { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0%); } to { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(min(100px, 5vh)); }
}

To apply animations we're going to have to know if the modal is opening or closing, this is where our setting of classnames comes in. We will always apply the modal class but we will only apply the closing class when the modal is not open.

// work out which classes should be applied to the dialog element
const dialogClasses = useMemo(() => { const _arr = [styles["modal"]]; if (!open) _arr.push(styles["modal--closing"]); return _arr.join(" ");
}, [open]);

Because the modal isn't closed when we remove the open attribute we can assume the modal is [open] but has the closing class.
We use this to apply the show animation when the modal is open and hide animation when the modal is open but has the closing class. We also use forwards as our animation direction so when the animation ends we stay on the last frame.

&[open] { animation: show 250ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) forwards; &.modal--closing { animation: hide 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) forwards; }
}

You may have noticed that with these changes our modal animates away but does not actually close, if you did, well done you're right and very astute. The next section will show you how we can use the animation to truly close the modal.

Capturing events

We have three things to look at here. Two of which are adding events and third is also adding an event but with a little trickery to allow us to close the modal with js, even though there isn't a method for it.

Closing on backdrop click

We can add a click event to the dialog element but there isn't a way to distinguish between clicking on the backdrop and clicking on the modal. The easiest way around this is to put a container inside the modal and have it take up the entire modal. Now when we click inside the modal the target will be the container and when we click outside the modal the target will be the dialog.

// Eventlistener: trigger onclose when click outside
const onClick = useCallback( ({ target }) => { const { current: el } = modalRef; if (target === el && !locked) onClose(); }, [locked, onClose]
);

Animating away on Escape

By default pressing escape closes the dialog, this is what we want to happen but unfortunately our animation would go with it so instead let's capture the escape press and deal with it ourselves.

// Eventlistener: trigger onclose when cancel detected
const onCancel = useCallback( (e) => { e.preventDefault(); if (!locked) onClose(); }, [locked, onClose]
);

Closing with JS

Both of the event listeners we've implemented so far call the onClose function which, as we discussed earlier, doesn't close the modal it just animates it away. In order to turn this animation into a method for closing we're going to need two things, hacky JSX and an event listeners.

Hacky JSX

The dialog element comes with a cool form integration, a button with the value cancel inside a form with the method dialog will close its parent dialog. That's a nice feature, couple that with the ability to hide elements with CSS, take them out of the tab order with tabIndex and the ability to click a button with JS and I think we have our close method.

<form method="dialog" className={styles["modal__close"]}> <button ref={closeRef} tabIndex={-1} value="cancel"> Cancel </button>
</form>

Now we just need to access our closeRef and run the click method on it and our dialog will close.

The Event listener

With the method for closing a dialog under our belt we can turn our attention to calling the method when our close animation is complete, fortunately when an animation completes it fires an event.

We'll need to check that our Modal element isn't open but provided it's not we can click our close button and just like that the dialog is closed.

// Eventlistener: trigger close click on anim end
const onAnimEnd = useCallback(() => { if (open) return; const { current: close } = closeRef; close.click();
}, [open]);

Closing thoughts

The web is changing and evolving everyday and it's exciting to keep up with what's going on. Thank you for reading and I hope you enjoyed learning about the dialog element and how to use it in React. If you have any questions, please feel free to ask them in the comments I'll be more than happy to answer them.

As I said at the top I really am proud of my original post about this topic so if you haven't read it and you're interested please head over there and have a look.

If you'd like to connect with me outside of Dev here are my twitter and linkedin come say hi 😊.
item.author: link2twenty

item.title: 使用 Amazon S3 + ImageKit.io 创建免费的图像大小调整 CDN | Create a Free Image Resizing CDN Using Amazon S3 + ImageKit.io
item.description:
并使用这些服务，我将向您展示如何结合使用这两种服务来创建图像优化和调整 CDN。

步骤 1：创建 AWS 账户和 S3 存储桶

我假设您可以自己弄清楚如何注册 Amazon Web Services 帐户。 不要因为需要提供信用卡而气馁。 除非您超出免费层级限制，否则不会向您的卡收费，并且 S3 存储桶的免费层级限制非常高。 此外，如果您确实超出了配额，您只需按需付费，而且费率非常低。

有关亚马逊简单存储服务定价的更多信息， 请单击此处。

好的，希望您现在拥有一个 AWS 账户，并且您的控制面板上有您的账户。 现在我们可以创建一个 S3 存储桶，我们将使用它来存储我们的图像文件。

单击仪表板右上角的服务图标，然后选择Storage->S3 。


单击仪表板右上角的服务图标，然后选择存储-> S3

接下来，输入存储桶名称并选择您的AWS 区域。 如果您在某个区域中还没有其他 AWS 服务，请选择一个地理位置接近您的区域。


输入存储桶名称并选择您的 AWS 区域

现在向下滚动并取消选中Block all public access 。 然后选中“我确认”复选框以继续。 保持所有其他设置不变，然后向下滚动并单击Create Bucket 。


向下滚动并取消选中阻止所有公共访问

好的，现在我们需要设置对 S3 存储桶的外部访问，以便 ImageKit.io 可以将其作为 CDN 边缘的外部源进行访问。

在服务图标旁边的搜索栏中，输入Iam ，然后单击IAM下的用户。


在服务图标旁边的搜索栏中，输入 Iam，然后单击 IAM 下的用户

然后单击添加用户并输入用户名。 然后选中Select AWS credential type下标记为Access key的框。


单击添加用户并输入用户名。 然后选中 Select AWS credential type 下标记为 Access key 的框

接下来，您需要将新用户添加到组中。 我们要创建组。 将组命名为您喜欢的任何名称，并在搜索栏中输入S3并检查AmazonS3FullAcess 。 这将使该组可以完全访问您的 S3 存储桶以管理对象。


单击将用户添加到组，然后搜索 S3 并检查 AmazonS3FullAccess

单击下一步，跳过添加标签，然后单击创建用户。 现在复制并粘贴您的访问密钥 ID和秘密访问密钥，并将它们存储在安全的地方。 关闭此屏幕后，您将无法再次看到您的密钥！

第 2 步：创建 ImageKit.io 帐户

前往ImageKit.io并创建您的免费帐户。 此服务不需要信用卡作为免费级别帐户，所以耶！ 另外，仅供参考：请使用我提供的链接，因为它附有我的附属链接。 如果你决定升级到付费计划，我得到 20 美元，你得到 10 美元。 但除非您想激活一些高级功能，否则您可能会使用免费帐户。 您可能希望在付费计划中使用的一项功能是在 ImageKit CDN 边缘和 S3 存储桶之间缓存调整大小的文件。

好的，现在你有一个 ImageKit.io 帐户，现在我们需要创建一个External Origin 。 单击左侧边栏上的外部存储，然后单击+ 添加新的。


单击左侧边栏上的外部存储，然后单击 + 添加新

接下来，输入一个友好的Origin name ，然后输入您的S3 存储桶名称、 S3 访问密钥和S3 密钥。 希望您将钥匙保存在安全的地方！ 对于S3 存储桶文件夹，您只需输入“/”即可。 如果要将根目录设置为特定文件夹，则可以。


输入您的 S3 存储桶名称、S3 访问密钥和 S3 密钥

现在将您的URL 端点复制并粘贴到新的浏览器选项卡中，并在斜杠后键入图像文件的相对路径。 你应该看到你的形象！ 伟大的！ 现在，您的图像正在由超快的 CDN 边缘提供服务！

现在，如果您想从 CDN 中检索调整大小的图像，您只需在文件前面添加一个路径，如下所示：

假设您的图像端点 URL 是https://ik.imagekit.io/r2d98yeyh9/images/some-image.jpg。 现在假设我们要将宽度调整为 500 像素。 我们只需这样做： https ://ik.imagekit.io/r2d98yeyh9/images/tr:w-300/some-image.jpg。

就是这样！ 有关转换选项的完整列表，以及做一些很酷的事情，例如命名转换，请阅读ImakeKit 文档。

我希望您发现本教程很有用。 如需更多重要信息和新信息，请访问我们的博客。



原文:

No longer is the realm of CDNs and automated image resizing accessible only to large corporations with deep wallets. Both Amazon Web Services and ImageKit.io have free-tier plans. And using these services, I will show you how to create an image-optimizing and -resizing CDN using these two services in conjunction.

Step 1: Create an AWS Account and an S3 Bucket

I’ll assume you can figure out how to register an Amazon Web Services account on your own. Don’t be discouraged by the need to give a credit card. Your card will not be charged unless you exceed free-tier limits, and the free-tier limits for an S3 bucket are exceedingly high. Also, if you do go over your quota, you only pay as you go and the rates are very low.

For more information on Amazon’s pricing for the Simple Storage Service, click here.

OK, so hopefully you now have an AWS account and your at your dashboard. Now we can create an S3 bucket, which we will use to store our image files.

Click the Services icon on the top right of the dashboard and select Storage->S3.


Click the Services icon on the top right of the dashboard and select Storage->S3

Next, enter a Bucket Name and choose your AWS Region. If you don’t already have other AWS services in a region, choose a region that this geographically close to you.


Enter a Bucket Name and choose your AWS Region

Now scroll down and uncheck Block all public access. Then check the “I Acknowledge” checkbox to continue. Leave all the other settings as-is and then scroll down and click Create Bucket.


Scroll down and uncheck Block all public access

OK, now we need to setup external access to the S3 bucket so ImageKit.io can access it as an external origin for your CDN edge.

In the Search Bar next to the Services icon type in Iam and then click Users under IAM.


In the Search Bar next to the Services icon type in Iam and then click Users under IAM

Then click Add Users and enter a username. Then check the box labeled Access key under Select AWS credential type.


Click Add Users and enter a username. Then check the box labeled Access key under Select AWS credential type

Next, you’ll need to add your new user to a group. We’re going to Create Group. Name the group whatever you like and the type S3 in the search baar and check AmazonS3FullAcess. This will give this group full access to your S3 bucket for managing objects.


Click Add User To Group and then search for S3 and check AmazonS3FullAccess

Click next, skip adding tags and then click Create User. Now copy and paste your Access key ID and Secret access key and store them in a safe place. Once you close this screen you will not be able to see your secret key again!

Step 2: Create an ImageKit.io Account

Head to ImageKit.io and create your free account. This service does not require a credit card for a free-tier account, so yay! Also, FYI: Please use the link I provided as it has my affiliate link attached. If you decide to upgrade to a paid plan, I get $20 and you get $10. But you’ll probably be staying with a free account unless you want to activate some advanced features. One feature you may want to use in a paid plan is caching of resized files between your ImageKit CDN edge and your S3 bucket.

OK, so now you have an ImageKit.io account, now we’ll need to create an External Origin. Click External Storage on the left sidebar and then click + Add New.


Click External Storage on the left sidebar and then click + Add New

Next, enter a friendly Origin name , and then enter your S3 bucket name , S3 access key , and S3 secret key. Hopefully you saved your keys in a safe place! For S3 bucket folder , you can simply enter “/”. If you want to set your root directory to a specific folder, you can.


Enter your S3 bucket name, S3 access key, and S3 secret key

Now copy and paste your URL Endpoint into a new browser tab and type the relative path to your image file after the slash. You should see your image! Great! Now you’re images are being served by a super-fast CDN edge!

Now, if you want to retrieve a resized image from your CDN, you simply have to prepend a path to your file as follows:

Let’s say your image endpoint URL is https://ik.imagekit.io/r2d98yeyh9/images/some-image.jpg. Now let’s say we want to resize the width to 500 pixels. We simply do this: https://ik.imagekit.io/r2d98yeyh9/images/tr:w-300/some-image.jpg.

That’s it! For a full list of transformation options, as well as doing cool things like Named Transformations , please read the ImakeKit Docs.

I hope you found this tutorial useful. For more great information and new, please visit Our Blog.
item.author: designly

item.title: 奔赴前线！ | To the frontline!
item.description:
大家好！ 这是 Hossomi，一位尝试学习前端的 Java 后端开发人员。 可能有很多人在讲述他们自己的类似旅程的故事，而我的只是其中之一。 但无论如何，分享不会受到伤害，甚至有人可能会喜欢！

在本系列中，我将尝试使用我不太熟悉的语言、库和技术来构建和部署全栈 Web 应用程序。 我不会因为复杂性而疯狂，因为它更像是一种练习，而不是任何严肃的事情，但我会借此机会尽可能多地学习并分享一路上的经验。

在第一篇文章中，我将介绍我自己、我的动机和经历。 最后，让我们考虑一下要构建什么！

我已经做了几年后端开发人员了，我还是很满意的。 自从我开始我的技术生涯以来，我使用过各种编程语言（主要但不仅限于 Java）、框架、工具、服务和其他技术。 总是有很多东西要学，还有很多可能性！

作为爱好编码的人之一，我有一些个人项目，我必须在其中处理数据库、Web API、部署等。 然而，有一个方面我什至无法做出改变：GUI。 在浏览器中运行的 Web 应用程序现在很流行，并且只能通过我几乎零知识的技术来实现。

我的技能组合中的这种差距感觉非常严重！ 任何需要前端技能的项目，无论是否是个人的，通常都是我无法企及的。 所以我决定是时候填补这个空白了。

这不是我第一次学习前端。 几年前，我想申请帮助我哥哥管理他的万智牌收藏。

我认为桌面应用程序会是最好的，所以环顾四周，我遇到了Electron 。 当时我不知道任何 JavaScript 或其任何框架和库，但这可能是一个学习的好机会，对吧？ 好吧，我很快就发现 JavaScript 生态系统可以荒谬地压倒一切。

通过教程安装库并在屏幕上显示内容并不困难。 但是我不得不走很多路！ 突然间，我开始学习 JavaScript、React、Redux、Webpack、Babel 和一堆其他同时出现的东西。 最终，我克隆了一个包含所有这些预配置的样板存储库，但它太大了，我什至无法理解。

我能够稍微脱离地面，学习每一种技术的一点点，并让一些东西在屏幕上发挥作用。 但是，在某个时候，我意识到我在盲目地更改代码，直到它几乎无法按我想要的那样工作。 缺乏对基本面的理解变得越来越没有生产力和动力。 代码如此纠缠，以至于现在尝试正确学习非常困难。 也许是时候退后一步，从小处着手。

在接下来的几年里，我有机会在更小的范围内再次与前端一起工作。 我了解了Create React App (CRA)，这是一个非常适合初学者的工具，它封装了静态 Web 应用程序的许多开发方面。 有了 CRA，我不再需要担心 Webpack，例如，这已经是我的一大负担。

一路走来，我意识到我不需要 Redux 。 当然，我最终将不得不回来使用所有这些工具（或替代品）。 这种新方法适用于我当时正在开发的应用程序，让我可以专注于更少的事情。

让我们从一个非常高级的想法开始，了解我将要构建什么以及一些需要关注的技术。

我仍然想在万智牌 (MTG) 方面帮助我的兄弟，所以这里有一些背景。 当新套装即将发布时，一些本地商店会举办发布前的锦标赛。 这些卡片是事先发布的，因此玩家可以学习和准备，我哥哥为此制作了一个包含大量信息的电子表格。

我编写了一个 shell 脚本，它从MTG JSON中获取集合数据，并使用jq生成一个包含他需要的所有数据的 CSV，然后他使用他的公式和格式将其复制到他的电子表格中。 他不能运行脚本（不是程序员），所以我运行它并给他 CSV。 将其变成更灵活、更自助的 Web 应用程序怎么样？

在技​​术方面，这些是我最初的想法。 当然，事情可能会在此过程中发生变化。

  • 打字稿。 我以前尝试过，以为我会从静态类型的主要编程语言中轻松适应，但我错了。 这次我没有要求给我压力，所以我相信我现在能够正确地学习它。 不过，我可能不会太严格，这取决于它的进展情况。

  • 用于查询这些字段的更灵活的接口可能很有用，并且适合在 API 层尝试 GraphQL 的用例。

反应（和 CRA）。 我会坚持下去，因为我还有很多东西要学。 我也将有更多的时间和自由来学习和实验。

  • 我使用过以前版本的 Material UI 并喜欢它。 新的 v5 似乎略有不同，但我认为值得学习使用其背后的系统更快地开发 UI。

  • 纱。 工作区听起来非常适合组织，即使我不会从应用程序中发布包。 赫罗库。 我所知道的最简单的应用程序平台，但功能非常强大。 我已经对其进行了几次测试，但从未深入过，我想了解更多关于它的功能。

    • 还有一些其他的开发方面我还没有想到，我会一路想出它们。 这足以让我们开始！

    保持 MTG 主题，我选择以 MTG 角色Tamiyo命名我们的应用程序。 她是一位在整个多元宇宙中寻找并记录知识的鹏洛客。 它有点符合这个应用程序的目的。

    查看GitHub 存储库（在撰写本文时为空）并继续关注下一篇文章！



    原文:

    Hello everyone! This is Hossomi, a Java backend developer trying to learn frontend. There probably is a multitude of people out there telling their own story of a similar journey, and mine is just one more of these. But anyway, sharing won't hurt, and someone might even enjoy!

    In this series, I will try to build and deploy a full-stack web application using languages, libraries and technologies I'm not very familiar with. I won't go wild on complexity because it's more of an exercise than anything serious, but I will take this chance to learn as much as I can and share the experience along the way.

    In this first post, I will introduce myself, my motivation and experiences. At the end, let's think a little about what to build!

    💡 Motivation

    I have been a backend developer for a few years now, and I am still very satisfied with it. Since I started my tech career, I have worked with various programming languages (mainly, but not only, Java), frameworks, tools, services and other technologies. There is always so much to learn and so many possibilities!

    As one of those people who also code as a hobby, I have personal projects in which I have to deal with databases, web APIs, deployments and such. However, there is one aspect I was never able to even make a dent: GUI. Web applications running in a browser are popular nowadays, and only attainable with technologies that I have nearly zero knowledge.

    This gap in my skillset feels very crippling! Any project, personal or not, that required frontend skills is usually out of my reach. So I decided it was time to fill that gap.

    🥉 The bad experience

    This is not going to be my first time learning frontend. A few years ago, I wanted to make an application to help my brother manage his Magic the Gathering collection.

    I thought that a desktop application would be best, so looking around I came across Electron. I didn't know any JavaScript or any of its frameworks and libraries at the time, but this could be a good opportunity to learn, right? Well, I quickly found out the hard way that JavaScript ecosystem can be absurdly overwhelming.

    Installing libraries and getting something on the screen is not difficult with a tutorial. But I had to go and overstep by a lot! Suddenly, I am learning JavaScript, React, Redux, Webpack, Babel and a bunch of other things that came along all at the same time. Eventually, I cloned a boilerplate repository with all these preconfigured, but it was so huge I couldn't even fathom.

    I was able to get slightly off the ground, learn a little of each of those technologies and get some things working on the screen. At some point, though, I realized I was blindly changing code until it barely worked as I wanted. The lack of understanding of the fundamentals was increasingly unproductive and demotivating. The code was so entangled that trying to learn properly now was difficult. Maybe it was time to step back and start smaller.

    🥈 The better experience

    During the next few years, I had the chance to work with frontend again in my job in a much smaller scope. I learned about Create React App (CRA), an amazing tool for beginners that encapsulates many development aspects for a static web application. With CRA, I no longer had to worry about Webpack, for example, which is already a huge weight off my shoulders.

    Along the way, I realized that I didn't need Redux. React's context API and hooks also came up to solve probably everything that I would use Redux for.

    Of course, I will have to come back for all these tools (or alternatives) eventually. This new approach worked for the applications I was working on at the time and allowed me to focus on fewer things.

    🥇 The next experience

    Let's start with a very high level idea of what I will build and a few technologies to focus on.

    I still want to help my brother with Magic the Gathering (MTG), so here's some background. When a new set is about to be released, some local stores hold pre-relase tournaments. The cards are published beforehand so players can study and prepare, and my brother builds a spreadsheet with lots of information for that.

    I wrote a shell script that fetches the set data from MTG JSON and generate a CSV using jq with all the data he needs, which he then copies over his spreadsheet with his formulae and formatting. He can't run the script (not a programmer) so I run it and give him the CSV. What about turning that into a more flexible, self-served web application?

    🎯 GOAL: display a set data from MTG JSON in a dynamic table and generate a CSV from it.

    On the technical side, these are what I have in mind initially. Of course things can change along the way.

    • Typescript. I have tried it before, thinking that I would adapt easily coming from a statically typed main programming language, but I was wrong. I don't have demands pressuring me this time, so I believe I will be able to learn it properly now. I might not be too strict, though, it depends on how it goes.

    • GraphQL. MTG JSON has quite a lot of data for each set and card. A more flexible interface for querying those fields might be useful, and suitable use case to try GraphQL out at the API layer.

    MTG JSON has a GraphQL API in beta available for supporters in case you are interested in such API (probably better than mine will be).

    • React (and CRA). I will stick to it since I still have much to learn. I will also have more time and freedom to study and experiment.

    • MUI Core. I have used previous versions of Material UI and enjoyed it. The new v5 seems slightly different, but I think it is worth learning to develop UI quicker with a system behind it.

    • Yarn. Workspaces sounds very nice for organization even though I won't be publishing packages out of the application. NPM is fine, I just think I can try something new here without shooting myself in the foot.

    • Heroku. The simplest application platform I know, yet very powerful. I have tested it a few times but never went too deep, I want to learn more about its capabilities.

    There are other development aspects I haven't even thought about yet, I will come up with them along the way. This is enough to get us started!

    🏁 Ready, set, go!

    Keeping the MTG theme, I chose to name our application after the MTG character Tamiyo. She is a planeswalker that seeks and records knowledge throughout the multiverse. It somewhat fits the purpose of this application.

    Check out the GitHub repository (at the time of this writing, empty) and stay tuned for next articles!
item.author: hossomi

item.title: 为什么设计模式对移动开发者如此重要！！ | Why Design patterns are so important to mobile devs!!
item.description:
克里斯托万·法里亚斯

发表于这些天我一直在想，对于移动开发人员来说，了解一些关于面向对象编程、干净代码和设计模式的原则有多重要。
了解这件事可以使开发人员的职业生涯增加很多，如果您至少了解设计模式以及如何以及何时使用它们，那么有很多工作需要知道如何使用我提到的东西的人，很可能你会成为技术面试的首选。

为了更清楚地说明这一点，看看我们如何在 Flutter 和 dart 中使用它，用 typescript、Kotlin、java 和 swift 做出本机反应。 所有这些语言总会有一些共同点，如果你有良好的编程基础，你就会拥有一切。

Eng Mouaz M. Al-Shahmeh - Eng Mouaz M. Al-Shahmeh - Mar 13 3月13日



原文:

Cristovão Farias

Posted on May 20

I was wondering and thinking a lot these days, how important is for a mobile developer to know some principles about Oriented object programming, clean code, and design patterns.
Learning about this thing can make a developer's career increase a lot, there are a ton of jobs that need someone that knows how to use the things that I mentioned if you have at least a knowledge about design patterns and how and when to use them, probably you will be the first choice in a technical interview.

To make this more clear, take a look at how we use this in flutter with dart, react native with typescript, Kotlin, java, and swift. All of those languages will always have something in common, if you have a good base with programming, you have everything.

Eng Mouaz M. Al-Shahmeh - Mar 13
item.author: cristovoxdgm

item.title: 如何使用ZEGOCLOUD SDK在android中实现Acoustic Echo Cancellation、Automatic Gain Control、Active Noise Control | How to use ZEGOCLOUD SDK to implement Acoustic Echo Cancellation、Automatic Gain Control、Active Noise Control in android
item.description:
发表于介绍

为了提升音频质量和用户体验，ZEGOCLOUD 提供了使用 3A 技术处理音频的能力，包括 AEC、AGC 和 ANS。

  • 开启此功能后，SDK 会对采集到的音频数据进行过滤，去除回声。
  • 启用此功能后，声音会被放大，但音质会受到一定影响。
  • 启用此功能将使您的声音更清晰。

先决条件

在开始之前，请确保完成以下步骤：

设置音频 3A 处理

设置 AEC（声学回声消除）

要实现 AEC 功能，请执行以下操作：

  1. 要启用 AEC 功能，请调用enableAEC方法。

  2. 可选：要启用耳机场景的AEC功能，请调用enableHeadphoneAEC方法。

  3. 要设置 AEC 模式，请调用setAECMode模式。

以下示例代码用于将 AEC 设置为中等模式 ( ZegoAECMode.MEDIUM )：

 // Enable the AEC feature. engine . enableAEC ( true ); // Enable the AEC when using headphones. engine . enableHeadphoneAEC ( true ); // Set the AEC to medium mode (ZegoAECMode.MEDIUM). engine . setAECMode ( ZegoAECMode . MEDIUM );

设置 AGC（自动增益控制）

要启用 AGC，请调用enableAGC方法。 开启此功能后，SDK 可自动调整麦克风音量以适应近远拾音，保持音量稳定。

 // Enable the AGC feature. engine . enableAGC ( true );

设置 ANS（主动噪声控制）

要实现 ANS 功能，请执行以下操作：

  1. 要启用 ANS 功能，请调用enableANS方法。

  2. 可选：要控制或消除瞬态噪声，请调用enableTransientANS方法。 此功能可用于抑制打字键盘、桌子等的噪音。

  3. 要设置或调整 ANS 模式，请调用setANSMode方法。 以下示例代码用于将 ANS 设置为软模式 ( ZegoANSMode.SOFT )：

     // Enable the ANS feature. engine . enableANS ( true ); // Enable the transient ANS feature to control the transient noise. engine . enableTransientANS ( true ); // Set the ANS to soft mode (ZegoANSMode.SOFT). engine . setANSMode ( ZegoANSMode . SOFT );

    方法描述默认配置推荐配置enableAEC 启用/禁用 AEC 功能。 真的我们建议您在一般情况下使用默认设置。 enableHeadphoneAEC 为耳机场景启用 AEC。 错误的我们建议您启用此功能以进行群组聊天或玩游戏。 setAECMode 设置 AEC 模式。 我们建议您在一般情况下使用默认设置。 enableAGC 启用/禁用 AGC 功能。 真的
    • 在聊天场景中，使用默认设置。
    • 在音乐广播场景下，关闭AGC功能以保持原声。
    • 在大班、小班、1v1等教育场景下，开启AGC功能。
    enableANS 启用/禁用 ANS 功能。 真的我们建议您在一般情况下使用默认设置。 setANSMode 设置 ANS 模式。 我们建议您在一般情况下使用默认设置。

HARITH O ONIGEMO - HARITH O ONIGEMO - May 19 5月19日

Nombres Para Free Fire - Nombres Para Free Fire - May 19 5月19日



原文:

ZEGOCLOUD Dev for ZEGOCLOUD

Posted on May 20

Introduction

To promote audio quality and user experience, ZEGOCLOUD provides the ability to process audio with 3A technologies, including AEC, AGC, and ANS.

  • AEC (Acoustic Echo Cancellation): A method of removing the echo from the audio signal captured by the microphone. After this feature is enabled, the SDK will filter the collected audio data to remove the echo.
  • AGC (Automatic Gain Control): A feature that automatically adjusts the receiver's audio gain (volume). After this feature is enabled, the sound will be amplified, but the sound quality will be affected to some extent.
  • ANS (Active Noise Control): A set of audio technologies that actively cancel the ambient noise. Enabling this feature will make your voice clearer.

Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure you complete the following steps:

Set up audio 3A processing

Set up the AEC (Acoustic Echo Cancellation)

To implement the AEC feature, do the following:

  1. To enable the AEC feature, call the enableAEC method.

  2. Optional: To enable the AEC feature for the headphones scenario, call the enableHeadphoneAEC method.

  3. To set the AEC mode, call the setAECMode mode.

The following sample code is used for setting the AEC to the medium mode (ZegoAECMode.MEDIUM):

// Enable the AEC feature.
engine.enableAEC(true);
// Enable the AEC when using headphones.
engine.enableHeadphoneAEC(true);
// Set the AEC to medium mode (ZegoAECMode.MEDIUM).
engine.setAECMode(ZegoAECMode.MEDIUM);

Set up the AGC (Automatic Gain Control)

To enable the AGC, call the enableAGC method. After this function is turned on, the SDK can automatically adjust the Microphone Volume to adapt to near and far sound pickups and keep the Volume stable.

// Enable the AGC feature.
engine.enableAGC(true);

Set up the ANS (Active Noise Control)

To implement the ANS feature, do the following:

  1. To enable the ANS feature, call the enableANS method.

  2. Optional: To control or cancel the transient noise, call the enableTransientANS method. This feature can be used to suppress the noise of typing keyboard, table, etc.

  3. To set or adjust the ANS mode, call the setANSMode method. The SDK uses medium mode (ZegoANSMode.MEDIUM) by default.

The following sample code is used for setting the ANS to the soft mode (ZegoANSMode.SOFT):

// Enable the ANS feature.
engine.enableANS(true);
// Enable the transient ANS feature to control the transient noise.
engine.enableTransientANS(true);
// Set the ANS to soft mode (ZegoANSMode.SOFT).
engine.setANSMode(ZegoANSMode.SOFT);

Recommended configurations for 3A

The default and recommended configurations for audio 3A processing in the SDK are as follows:

Method Description Default config Recommended config
enableAEC Enable/Disable the AEC feature. True We recommend you use the default settings for general scenarios.
enableHeadphoneAEC Enable the AEC for the headphone scenario. False We recommend you enable this for chatting or playing games in groups.
setAECMode Set the AEC mode. ZegoAECMode.AGGRESSIVE (Aggressive mode) We recommend you use the default settings for general scenarios.
enableAGC Enable/Disable the AGC feature. True
  • In the chatting scenario, use the default settings.
  • In the music radio scenario, disable the AGC feature to keep original sound.
  • In the educational scenarios, such as large classes, small classes, and 1v1 classes, enable the AGC feature.
enableANS Enable/Disable the ANS feature. True We recommend you use the default settings for general scenarios.
setANSMode Set the ANS mode. ZegoANSMode.MEDIUM (Medium mode) We recommend you use the default settings for general scenarios.

HARITH O ONIGEMO - May 19

Nombres Para Free Fire - May 19
item.author: tanyaqxy_30

item.author: leo1612d

item.title: JS(ES6+) 开发者必须知道的 50 条提示（第三部分） | 50+ hints JS(ES6+) developer must know (3rd part)
item.description:
暴风雨天赋

发表于今天，我们将有更多使用 JS 的技巧。
字符串使用单引号 '' 表示字符串。 

 // bad const name = "Capt. Janeway"; // bad - template literals should contain interpolation or newlines const name = `Capt. Janeway`; // good const name = 'Capt. Janeway';

导致行超过 100 个字符的字符串不应使用字符串连接跨多行写入。

 // bad const errorMessage = 'This is a super long error that was thrown because \ of Batman. When you stop to think about how Batman had anything to do \ with this, you would get nowhere \ fast.'; // bad const errorMessage = 'This is a super long error that was thrown because ' + 'of Batman. When you stop to think about how Batman had anything to do ' + 'with this, you would get nowhere fast.'; // good const errorMessage = 'This is a super long error that was thrown because of Batman. When you stop to think about how Batman had anything to do with this, you would get nowhere fast.';

以编程方式构建字符串时，请使用模板字符串而不是串联。

 // bad function sayHi(name) { return 'How are you, ' + name + '?'; } // bad function sayHi(name) { return ['How are you, ', name, '?'].join(); } // bad function sayHi(name) { return `How are you, ${ name }?`; } // good function sayHi(name) { return `How are you, ${name}?`; }

不要不必要地转义字符串中的字符。

 // bad const foo = '\'this\' \i\s \"quoted\"'; // good const foo = '\'this\' is "quoted"'; const foo = `my name is '${name}'`;

函数使用命名函数表达式而不是函数声明。

 // bad function foo() { // ... } // bad const foo = function () { // ... }; // good // lexical name distinguished from the variable-referenced invocation(s) const short = function longUniqueMoreDescriptiveLexicalFoo() { // ... };

将立即调用的函数表达式包装在括号中。

 // immediately-invoked function expression (IIFE) (function () { console.log('Welcome to the Internet. Please follow me.'); }());

注意：ECMA-262 将块定义为语句列表。 函数声明不是语句。

 // bad if (currentUser) { function test() { console.log('Nope.'); } } // good let test; if (currentUser) { test = () => { console.log('Yup.'); }; }

永远不要命名参数参数。 这将优先于赋予每个函数范围的参数对象。

 // bad function foo(name, options, arguments) { // ... } // good function foo(name, options, args) { // ... }

永远不要使用参数，而是选择使用 rest 语法 ...。

 // bad function concatenateAll() { const args = Array.prototype.slice.call(arguments); return args.join(''); } // good function concatenateAll(...args) { return args.join(''); }

使用默认参数语法而不是改变函数参数。

 // really bad function handleThings(opts) { // No! We shouldn't mutate function arguments. // Double bad: if opts is falsy it'll be set to an object which may // be what you want but it can introduce subtle bugs. opts = opts || {}; // ... } // still bad function handleThings(opts) { if (opts === void 0) { opts = {}; } // ... } // good function handleThings(opts = {}) { // ... }

使用默认参数避免副作用。

 var b = 1; // bad function count(a = b++) { console.log(a); } count(); // 1 count(); // 2 count(3); // 3 count(); // 3

始终将默认参数放在最后。

 // bad function handleThings(opts = {}, name) { // ... } // good function handleThings(name, opts = {}) { // ... }

永远不要使用 Function 构造函数来创建新函数。

 // bad var add = new Function('a', 'b', 'return a + b'); // still bad var subtract = Function('a', 'b', 'return a - b');

函数签名中的间距。

 // bad const f = function(){}; const g = function (){}; const h = function() {}; // good const x = function () {}; const y = function a() {};

永远不要改变参数。

 // bad function f1(obj) { obj.key = 1; } // good function f2(obj) { const key = Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(obj, 'key') ? obj.key : 1; }

永远不要重新分配参数。

 // bad function f1(a) { a = 1; // ... } function f2(a) { if (!a) { a = 1; } // ... } // good function f3(a) { const b = a || 1; // ... } function f4(a = 1) { // ... }

更喜欢使用扩展语法 ... 来调用可变参数函数。

 // bad const x = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; console.log.apply(console, x); // good const x = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; console.log(...x); // bad new (Function.prototype.bind.apply(Date, [null, 2016, 8, 5])); // good new Date(...[2016, 8, 5]);

具有多行签名或调用的函数应该像本指南中的所有其他多行列表一样缩进：每个项目单独一行，最后一个项目尾随逗号。

 // bad function foo(bar, baz, quux) { // ... } // good function foo( bar, baz, quux, ) { // ... } // bad console.log(foo, bar, baz); // good console.log( foo, bar, baz, );

谢谢你的时间。 希望这篇博客能给你的发展带来强烈的启发。

下一次，我将在 JS 中部署箭头函数。

HARITH O ONIGEMO - HARITH O ONIGEMO - May 19 5月19日

Nombres Para Free Fire - Nombres Para Free Fire - May 19 5月19日



原文:

StormyTalent

Posted on May 20

Today, We are going to have some more tips in usage of JS.
String Use single quotes '' for strings.

// bad
const name = "Capt. Janeway";

// bad - template literals should contain interpolation or newlines
const name = `Capt. Janeway`;

// good
const name = 'Capt. Janeway';

Strings that cause the line to go over 100 characters should not be written across multiple lines using string concatenation.

// bad
const errorMessage = 'This is a super long error that was thrown because \
of Batman. When you stop to think about how Batman had anything to do \
with this, you would get nowhere \
fast.';

// bad
const errorMessage = 'This is a super long error that was thrown because ' +
  'of Batman. When you stop to think about how Batman had anything to do ' +
  'with this, you would get nowhere fast.';

// good
const errorMessage = 'This is a super long error that was thrown because of Batman. When you stop to think about how Batman had anything to do with this, you would get nowhere fast.';

When programmatically building up strings, use template strings instead of concatenation.

// bad
function sayHi(name) {
  return 'How are you, ' + name + '?';
}

// bad
function sayHi(name) {
  return ['How are you, ', name, '?'].join();
}

// bad
function sayHi(name) {
  return `How are you, ${ name }?`;
}

// good
function sayHi(name) {
  return `How are you, ${name}?`;
}

Do not unnecessarily escape characters in strings.

// bad
const foo = '\'this\' \i\s \"quoted\"';

// good
const foo = '\'this\' is "quoted"';
const foo = `my name is '${name}'`;

Functions Use named function expressions instead of function declarations.

// bad
function foo() {
  // ...
}

// bad
const foo = function () {
  // ...
};

// good
// lexical name distinguished from the variable-referenced invocation(s)
const short = function longUniqueMoreDescriptiveLexicalFoo() {
  // ...
};

Wrap immediately invoked function expressions in parentheses.

// immediately-invoked function expression (IIFE)
(function () {
  console.log('Welcome to the Internet. Please follow me.');
}());

Note: ECMA-262 defines a block as a list of statements. A function declaration is not a statement.

// bad
if (currentUser) {
  function test() {
    console.log('Nope.');
  }
}

// good
let test;
if (currentUser) {
  test = () => {
    console.log('Yup.');
  };
}

Never name a parameter arguments. This will take precedence over the arguments object that is given to every function scope.

// bad
function foo(name, options, arguments) {
  // ...
}

// good
function foo(name, options, args) {
  // ...
}

Never use arguments, opt to use rest syntax ... instead.

// bad
function concatenateAll() {
  const args = Array.prototype.slice.call(arguments);
  return args.join('');
}

// good
function concatenateAll(...args) {
  return args.join('');
}

Use default parameter syntax rather than mutating function arguments.

// really bad
function handleThings(opts) {
  // No! We shouldn’t mutate function arguments.
  // Double bad: if opts is falsy it'll be set to an object which may
  // be what you want but it can introduce subtle bugs.
  opts = opts || {};
  // ...
}

// still bad
function handleThings(opts) {
  if (opts === void 0) {
    opts = {};
  }
  // ...
}

// good
function handleThings(opts = {}) {
  // ...
}

Avoid side effects with default parameters.

var b = 1;
// bad
function count(a = b++) {
  console.log(a);
}
count();  // 1
count();  // 2
count(3); // 3
count();  // 3

Always put default parameters last.

// bad
function handleThings(opts = {}, name) {
  // ...
}

// good
function handleThings(name, opts = {}) {
  // ...
}

Never use the Function constructor to create a new function.

// bad
var add = new Function('a', 'b', 'return a + b');

// still bad
var subtract = Function('a', 'b', 'return a - b');

Spacing in a function signature.

// bad
const f = function(){};
const g = function (){};
const h = function() {};

// good
const x = function () {};
const y = function a() {};

Never mutate parameters.

// bad
function f1(obj) {
  obj.key = 1;
}

// good
function f2(obj) {
  const key = Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(obj, 'key') ? obj.key : 1;
}

Never reassign parameters.

// bad
function f1(a) {
  a = 1;
  // ...
}

function f2(a) {
  if (!a) { a = 1; }
  // ...
}

// good
function f3(a) {
  const b = a || 1;
  // ...
}

function f4(a = 1) {
  // ...
}

Prefer the use of the spread syntax ... to call variadic functions.

// bad
const x = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
console.log.apply(console, x);

// good
const x = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
console.log(...x);

// bad
new (Function.prototype.bind.apply(Date, [null, 2016, 8, 5]));

// good
new Date(...[2016, 8, 5]);

Functions with multiline signatures, or invocations, should be indented just like every other multiline list in this guide: with each item on a line by itself, with a trailing comma on the last item.

// bad
function foo(bar,
             baz,
             quux) {
  // ...
}

// good
function foo(
  bar,
  baz,
  quux,
) {
  // ...
}

// bad
console.log(foo,
  bar,
  baz);

// good
console.log(
  foo,
  bar,
  baz,
);

Thanks for your time. I hope this blog will give you strong inspirations for your development.

Next time, I will deploy about the Arrow Functions in JS.

HARITH O ONIGEMO - May 19

Nombres Para Free Fire - May 19

item.title: 京东的困境 | JD's Dilemma
item.description:
这不仅仅是一个故事。 我目前正在经历我应该采取行动的想法的爆发。

在我们陷入困境之前，先对我做一点介绍。 我是一名大学学生，他真的很好奇，每当他了解一些很酷的技术/主题时，他都想深入研究，只想深入了解它的底层，了解它的工作原理和你可以用它做的很酷的事情。

我通常对多个主题和技术感兴趣的频率比你想象的要多，但是有些我的想法不太清楚，并写了接下来几个月我必须学习和关注的东西。

一切都很顺利，直到有一天我决定参加有关开发人员生产力的课程（我将对讲师和课程进行单独的审查，这真是太棒了:JOY: ：）

如果你不知道我是 gnu linux 的忠实粉丝。 当我发现自己在观看视频和阅读文章以安装 gentoo/arch 以及 WM、ricing 时，我已经停止了很多次，而且你知道列表还在继续。

问题是出于某种原因，我想要完美的东西，比如完美的点文件、管理它们的方式、Nvim 配置和列表继续。 你可以用 Linux 做很多事情。

有趣的事实：您在上面看到的图像是一只 Gentoo 企鹅（也许您不是 linux 高级用户，有 linux 发行版 [基于源代码] 称为 gentoo linux）它是最快的企鹅，您可以得到类比。

您可能想知道这一切与课程有什么关系？

答案是该课程教授如何以最佳方式设置您的开发人员环境，以及如何在任何新机器上以几分之一秒的方式复制相同的环境。猜猜这一切都是从 docker 容器和 Ansible 开始的，以自动化安装当您在一个全新的系统上并且想要与您自己的机器相同的设置时需要 pkgs。

现在我需要学习 docker :JOY:并从 Ansible 开始。 我问自己的下一个问题是关于 OS、wm、bar 等。我忽略了这个想法并继续进行下一个会话，他解决了另一个痛点，即 dotfiles。

然后他谈到了tmux（我一直在想为什么，直到现在才发现逃跑很复杂）另一个痛点。 然后他谈到了 wm、bash、git 等。我了解所有这些，并且经常使用 cli。 但他使用它们的方式真是太棒了。 我的大脑就像让我们现在就去做，成为一名精英，但现实是不同的。

当我开始弄清楚东西时，我再次发现自己陷入了完美的兔子洞。 我想获得完美的 wm（如果你对我在说什么一无所知，只需 google windows manger）安装，为此，当我在 vim 中打开它时，我现在想首先拥有在 vim 上操作的完美技能和它的配置。

是时候让我以不同的视角看待问题了，让我在完成工作的同时保持快乐，而不必担心完美。 我真的觉得写下来真的可以帮助我理清思路，我将走上成长的道路。
这是我的第一个博客，唯一的原因是记录我的旅程，弄清楚我遇到的困难，希望

事实上，在开始写博客之前，我想在最好的平台上搜索很多，并学习写博客/写作（你看这个兔子洞不会离开我:JOY:但我限制了我的搜索观看了 2 个视频并开始了它。

所以我的旅程开始了，将记录我在野外所做的大部分事情。

关键要点

  1. 获得清晰
  2. 在叶子的生命周期中，您会称其为完美。 第一步总是很艰难，所以走出你的舒适区。


原文:

It's not just a story. I'm currently going through the burst of thoughts on which I should act.

A little intro about me before we dive into the dilemma thing. I'm a student at college who is really curious that whenever he get to know about some cool technology/topic he wants to dive deep and just wanna go through it's bottom and understand it's working and cool stuff you can do with it.

I usually get interested in multiple topics and tech more often then you can think of, But Some how I got my thoughts little clear and wrote things I had to learn and focus on for next few months.

Everything was going smooth until one day I decided to take course on developer productivity(I will do a separate review on the instructor and course just blazingly amazing :JOY:)

If you don't know I'm big fan of gnu linux. Linux is sort of thing I would spent my all time on once I start. I've stopped myself so many times when I find myself watching videos and reading articles to install gentoo/arch along with WM, ricing and you know list goes on.

The problems is for some reason I want things perfect like perfect dotfiles, the way you manage them, Nvim config and again list goes on. There is plethora of things you can do with Linux.

fun fact: the image you see above is of a Gentoo penguin(maybe you aren't a linux power user there is linux distro[source based] called gentoo linux) It's the fastest penguin and you can get the analogy.

You might be wondering what does this all have to do with course?

The answer is the course taught how to set up your developer Environment best way possible and how to replicate same environment the chad way in fractions of seconds on any new machine.And guess what It all started with docker container and Ansible to automate the installation of required pkgs when you are on a brand new system and want the same setup as your own machine.

Now I'm like need to learn docker :JOY: and start with Ansible. Next question I asked my self was what about OS, wm, bar etc. I ignored the thought and moved on to the next session and he addressed another pain point which is dotfiles.

then he talked about tmux(I always thought why and found it complex to run away until now) another pain point. then he talked about wm, bash, git etc. I knew all of 'em and use cli a lot. but the way he used 'em was just gorgeous. My brain was like let's do it all right now and be an Elitist but reality was different.

When I started to figure out stuff I again found myself in rabbit hole of perfection. I wanted to get perfect wm(just google windows manger if you don't have any idea on what I'm talking about) installation and for that when I open it in vim I now want to first have perfect skills to operate on vim and it's configurations.

It's time I look at the problem with a different perspective which gets me things done and be happy at same time without worrying about perfection. I really feel like writing things down can really help me clear out my thoughts and I will be on a path of growth.
This is my first blog and sole reason is to document my journey, get clear on aspects I struggle with, hopefully

In fact, before starting a blog I wanted to search a lot on which is best platform and learn to write blog/writing in general(you see this rabbit hole doesn't leave me :JOY:. but I limited my search watched 2 videos and started it.

So my journey begins, will be documenting most of the things I do in the wild.

key takeaways

  1. Get clarity
  2. When in the lifespan of leaf you would call its perfect. (answer is its always perfect and that's the beauty so stop worrying about perfection and just do it)
  3. First step is always hard so get out of your comfort zone.
item.author: jdranpariya

item.title: Cory Carnley 是一位热心的雪茄爱好者 | Cory Carnley is a dedicated cigar enthusiast
item.description:
科里·卡恩利 ( Cory Carnley ) 是一位忠实的雪茄迷，拥有烟草商、调酒师和负责任的自动售货员的资质。 此外，他还曾在烟草行业工作。

卡恩利在年轻时就对烟草产生了浓厚的兴趣，但在他十八岁时开始蓬勃发展，并迅速连续抽了两支雪茄作为庆祝行为。 此后不久，他成为美国雪茄权利组织历史上最年轻的成员，该组织是优质雪茄业务的首要游说组织。

科里·卡恩利 ( Cory Carnley ) 在使用烟草产品期间曾尝试过优质雪茄、优质小雪茄、“加油站”小雪茄、香烟、烟斗、水烟、咀嚼烟草、蘸烟草和鼻烟。



原文:

Cory Carnley is a devoted cigar fan who possesses credentials as a tobacconist, in bartending, and in responsible vending. In addition, he has worked in the tobacco industry.

Carnley's interest in tobacco was aroused while he was young, but it began to flourish when he reached eighteen and smoked two cigars in rapid succession as a celebratory act. Soon after that, he became the youngest member in the history of the Cigar Rights of America, which is the premier lobbying organization for the premium cigar business.

Cory Carnley has, and still does, devote a significant amount of time and effort to conducting research on and experimenting with a wide range of tobacco products and accessories in order to educate himself on the many different aspects of the tobacco industry.

Cory Carnley has tried premium cigars, premium cigarillos, "gas station" cigarillos, cigarettes, pipes, hookah, chewing tobacco, dipping tobacco, and nasal snuff during his time with tobacco products.
item.author: corycarnley

item.title: 11 款适合经理的最佳生产力应用程序 | 11 Best Productivity Apps for Managers
item.description:
如果你是一名经理，你可以放心地打赌，每一天都会为你的团队带来新的挑战。 但是，随着工作量的增加，自大流行开始以来，会议增加了 37.9% ，并且跨远程或混合工作设置协调直接下属 -并且无法理解经理们在哪里找到时间来完成他们自己的生产性任务工作.

生产力是完成目标的工作和时间的效率水平。 而对于管理人员来说，他们几乎没有50% 的任务时间实际上花在了高效、专注的工作上。 每周工作时间只有 40 小时（是的，没错！）——经理们迫切需要一种解决方案，以便在工作日的有限时间中再挤出几分钟。 幸运的是，有许多不同的生产力工具可以帮助管理人员减轻一些繁重的工作，这样他们就可以专注于与团队一起推动真正的价值。

好消息 - 我们已经研究和分析了数百种工具， 为管理者编制了 11 款最佳生产力应用程序。 在本文中，我们比较了每个应用程序如何在他们最需要的领域帮助管理人员，包括计划、时间管理和沟通 - 涵盖收益与潜在的缺点和定价。

管理者在生产力工具中需要什么？

经理是组织中的团队领导者——创建项目计划、委派任务和优化团队的效率。 但是大辞职带来了新的挑战。 由于任务负载增加、团队比以往任何时候都更忙，以及完成所有工作的时间更少，经理们现在面临着重大的生产力困难。

我们的2022 年任务管理趋势报告揭示了许多关于经理生产力和时间管理的有趣统计数据：

  • 经理平均每天花费1.83 小时在生产性任务工作上，或每周 9.2 小时。
  • 平均经理每天花费3.6 小时在他们自己的任务工作上，但其中 1.82 小时用于非生产性任务工作。
  • 经理每周花费 5 小时为他们的团队分配、优先排序和重新排序工作。
  • 只有26.9% 的管理者平均每天工作超过 4 小时，9% 的管理者平均每天超过 6 小时。
  • 平均而言，只有10.9% 的经理在超过 70% 的任务时间内保持生产力。
  • 在 1-10 的范围内，平均经理将自己保护团队免受干扰和分心的能力评为 5.3 分。

结果表明，许多经理都在努力为他们的团队和他们自己争取每周埋头工作的时间。 毫不奇怪， 71.4% 的经理也感到压力，这要归功于任务和非生产性会议的增加，以及缺乏时间深入研究他们的高价值项目。 让我们分解一些经理的最大时间管理目标：

  • 保护你的时间不被超支，这样你就可以处理你的优先事项。
  • 安排好你的时间，这样你就知道你在做什么，什么时候做。
  • 在有限的专注时间内，尽量减少分心，以最大限度地提高工作效率。
  • 优化工作流程以节省时间并提高效率。
  • 简化沟通以更好地签到、安排和委派。**

不用说，这些时间管理目标很难靠自己来解决，但幸运的是，市场上有大量出色的生产力应用程序可以帮助您和您的团队节省时间。

超过600 万家企业使用Google Workspace （前身为 G Suite），并受到全球一些大公司的信赖。 作为一名平衡各种职责的经理，Google Workspace 是一个很棒的基础系统，可让您与团队进行沟通、协作和创造。 您还可以使用您的 Google 帐户 SSO 注册并登录到许多不同的平台，以获得高级安全和保护（以及更少的密码来跟踪）。

面向经理的主要 Google Workspace 功能：

  • 保护您的时间：手动阻止专注于日历上的低头工作。
  • 安排您的时间：通过为您想要完成的事情创建事件来计划您的工作日，并从 Gmail 将新事件添加到您的日历中。
  • 尽量减少干扰：通过聊天关闭通知，以便在专注时间减少干扰。
  • 优化工作流程：所有 Google 应用程序都原生集成了用户标记、评论、批准或拒绝编辑、访问链接共享、项目协作等功能，并且“查找时间”功能可让您快速查看与您的联系人联系的最佳时间团队。
  • 简化沟通：通过本地聊天、电子邮件和视频会议功能联系您的团队，让您始终保持联系。

潜在的缺点：虽然谷歌日历在组织你的时间和可视化你一周的时间段方面非常棒，但它不能在发生冲突或优先级改变时提供自动化，这会导致当你必须重新确定工作周的优先级时需要大量手动操作.

免费试用： 14 天
价格：计划起价为 6 美元/月
平台： Web、iOS 和 Android

这款生产力应用程序通过灵活和自适应的时间限制节省多达 40% 的工作时间，并通过实际自动化您无休止的待办事项和日历之间的差距来检查我们所有的经理生产力目标 - 所以您总是有时间做最多的事情重要的事情在你的盘子里，可以平衡你在会议上的时间。

面向经理的顶级 Reclaim.ai 功能：

  • 保护您的时间：从您的项目管理应用程序（ClickUp、Todoist、Jira、Linear、Google Tasks）中集成您的任务列表，以自动为您的待办事项安排时间，并为您的日常工作设置重复的智能时间块。
  • 安排您的时间：自动安排您的日程安排，围绕哪些任务或例程对您来说是最高优先级，并在优先级发生变化时自动重新安排。
  • 尽量减少干扰：通过时间限制为您的日历添加更多上下文，并将您的 Slack 状态同步到 Google 日历和 Zoom，让您的同事知道您何时很忙且不能被打扰（例如在与直接下属的 1 对 1 重要会议期间） ）。
  • 优化工作流程：通过集成自动为您的团队分配时间来处理任务分配，并根据团队的实际可用性通过现实的估计来改进您的每周冲刺计划。
  • 简化沟通：使用 Smart 1:1 自动安排重复的一对一会议，这样您就不必再浪费时间来回安排时间了。

潜在缺点： Reclaim 目前仅适用于 Google 日历（2022 年产品路线图上的 Outlook），因此团队在 Google Workspace 系统上运行的经理将从这款生产力应用程序中获益最多。

免费试用： 14 天
价格：免费，付费计划起价为 6.50 美元/月
平台：网络

您还可以添加多个日历以避免跨日程重复预订。

  • 安排您的时间：创建会议类型以围绕您想要参加某些会议（例如周一和周三的销售会议以及周二和周四的用户会议）来组织您的工作周。
